SEARCH
Package kf.api

Class Api

java.lang.Object
kf.api.Api
public class Api extends Object
The Api class handles authentication and communication with the Fortnox API. It provides functionality for sending invoices and managing authentication tokens. This class requires an env.txt file containing: - Client ID (line 1) - Client Secret (line 2) - Auth URL (line 3) - Redirect URL (line 4) The class manages: - OAuth 2.0 authentication flow - Access token retrieval and management - Invoice creation and cancellation - Batch processing of invoices with rate limiting Important notes: - The class automatically starts a temporary local server for authentication - Rate limiting is implemented (25 requests per 5 seconds) for batch operations - Failed batch operations will automatically cancel all successfully created invoices
Version:
1.0
Author:
Your Name
See Also:
  • Invoice
  • LocalServer

  • Constructor Details

    • Api

      public Api()
      Constructs an Api instance with the defualt configuration file.

    • Api

      public Api(File file)
      Constructs an Api instance with a specified configuration file.
      Parameters:
      file - The environment configuration file to be used for setting up the API This allows for different configurations during testing.

  • Method Details

    • sendInvoice

      public boolean sendInvoice(Invoice invoice) throws IOException, InterruptedException
      Sends an invoice to the Fortnox API using the provided Invoice object. This method serializes the Invoice object to JSON and sends a POST request to the Fortnox API. The response is checked for success and the method returns a boolean indicating the result.
      Parameters:
      invoice - The Invoice object to be sent to the Fortnox API
      Returns:
      true if the invoice was successfully sent, false otherwise
      Throws:
      IOException - if the request fails
      InterruptedException - if the request is interrupted

    • sendInvoiceList

      public int sendInvoiceList(List<Invoice> invoices) throws IOException, InterruptedException
      Sends a list of invoices to the Fortnox API using the provided List of Invoice objects. This method serializes each Invoice object to JSON and sends a POST request to the Fortnox API. The response is checked for success and the method returns the number of invoices successfully sent.
      Parameters:
      invoices - The List of Invoice objects to be sent to the Fortnox API
      Returns:
      the number of invoices successfully sent
      Throws:
      IOException - if the request fails
      InterruptedException - if the request is interrupted

    • sendInvoiceTest

      public HttpResponse<String> sendInvoiceTest(Invoice invoice)
      Sends a test invoice to the Fortnox API using the provided Invoice object. This method serializes the Invoice object to JSON and sends a POST request to the Fortnox API. The response is checked for success and the method returns the HttpResponse object.
      Parameters:
      invoice - The Invoice object to be sent to the Fortnox API
      Returns:
      the HttpResponse object containing the response from the Fortnox API

    • removeInvoice

      public boolean removeInvoice(String invoiceNumber) throws IOException, InterruptedException
      Removes an invoice from the Fortnox API using the provided invoice number. This method sends a PUT request to the Fortnox API to cancel the invoice with the specified number. The response is checked for success and the method returns a boolean indicating the result.
      Parameters:
      invoiceNumber - The invoice number of the invoice to be removed
      Returns:
      true if the invoice was successfully removed, false otherwise
      Throws:
      IOException - if the request fails
      InterruptedException - if the request is interrupted

    • close

      public void close()
      Closes the local server used for authentication.
